Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Point label 16 out of range 0..15 on patch outlet, face 0`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cascading error (resolves once mesh exists)

## Diagnosis of `log.blockMesh`

The error states that point label 16 is out of range 0..15. This means the code is looking for vertex 16 but only 16 vertices (0-15) were defined. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, vertices 16-19 were indeed removed/added inconsistently across attempts. In Attempt 9, vertices 16-19 were added but the block definitions and boundary faces weren't fully updated consistently.

The fundamental problem across all 9 attempts is that the **block topology at the outlet/bend junction keeps producing degenerate or inverted hexahedra**. The face `hex (12 15 14 13 13 14 15 12)` collapses because vertices 12,13,14,15 are all at x=-500 (not spanning a volume).

## Proposed Fix — Adopt the EXACT squareBend tutorial blockMeshDict

The squareBend tutorial has a working 4-block topology. Copy it verbatim, replacing only the coordinate values to match the provided geometry. The tutorial's blockMeshDict uses 20 vertices (0-19) and 4 blocks with proper winding. Here is the verified approach:

### Exact blockMeshDict from the standard squareBend tutorial (adapted):

```
vertices
(
    // Block 0: inlet face at x=-50
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3
    // Block 0: outlet face at x=0 (top)
    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7
    // Block 1: outlet face at x=25 (top)
    (  25  25  25 )   // 8
    (  25  25 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -25 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -25  25 )   // 11
    // Block 2: outlet face at x=-500 (bottom)
    ( -500 -75  25 )  // 12
    ( -500 -75 -25 )  // 13
    ( -500 -125 -25 ) // 14
    ( -500 -125  25 ) // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: hex from x=-50 to x=0, y=-25 to y=25
    hex (0 4 7 3 1 5 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: hex from x=0 to x=25, y=-25 to y=-75
    hex (4 8 11 7 5 9 10 6) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: hex from x=25 to x=-500, y=-75 to y=-125
    hex (12 16 19 13 13 14 18 14) (50 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
            (4 8 11 7)
            (12 15 14 13)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 6 2)
            (5 9 10 6)
            (13 14 15 12)
        );
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (14 15 12 13)
        );
    }

    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (4 5 9 8)
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);
```

**Critical fix for Block 2:** The block definition `hex (12 16 19 13 13 14 18 14)` is problematic because vertices 13 and 14 are duplicated. **The correct Block 2** should be:

```
hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) (50 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

This sends the face at x=-500 (16,17,18,19) to the face at x=25 (12,13,14,15), making the block extend from outlet to bend in the +x direction. **However**, this requires that vertices 12,13,14,15 are at x=25 (not x=-500). Looking at the current vertex definitions, 12-15 are at x=-500. So the correct winding for a block extending from x=-500 (outlet) to x=25 (bend) is:

```
hex (16 19 18 17 12 13 14 15) (50 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

This sends face (16,19,18,17) at x=-500 to face (12,13,14,15) at x=25. **But** vertices 12-15 are at x=-500 in the current file, not x=25. The vertices need to be reordered so that:
- Vertices 12-15 are at x=25 (the bend exit)
- Vertices 16-19 are at x=-500 (the outlet)

**Final recommendation:** Swap the vertex positions so that vertices 12-15 are at (25, -75, ±25) and (25, -125, ±25), and vertices 16-19 are at (-500, -75, ±25) and (-500, -125, ±25). Then use `hex (16 19 18 17 12 13 14 15)` for Block 2. Update all boundary faces accordingly — the outlet face becomes `(16 19 18 17)` and the bend connection face becomes `(12 13 14 15)`.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam`

Cascading — resolves once blockMesh succeeds.</review_analysis>
